title: achillea millefolium subsp. borealis
intro: Achillea millefolium subsp. borealis, also known as boreal yarrow or northern yarrow, is a subspecies of the perennial herb yarrow (Achillea millefolium), characterized by its fern-like foliage and white or pinkish flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, reducing pain and fever, and preventing the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms. The health benefits of boreal yarrow include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce menstrual cramps, and lower blood pressure, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Boreal yarrow can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in traditional medicine, aromatherapy, and as a natural remedy for various health conditions.
